subject: [PATCH] mm/mempolicy: Fix weighted interleave auto sysfs name

reviews.author: Huang, Ying
reviews.summary: Reviewer requested that Fixes tag be added to the commit message for easier backporting and suggested adding Acked-by: Huang Ying in future versions
reviews.raw_body:
Fixes tag helps people to decide whether to backport the commit.  So, I
think that we need it anyway.  Feel free to add

Acked-by: Huang Ying <ying.huang@linux.alibaba.com>

in the future versions.

[snip]

---
Best Regards,
Huang, Ying

2026-04-09.patch_summary: This patch fixes a naming issue in the weighted interleave auto sysfs attribute by directly passing a string 'auto' instead of the raw token, which was being expanded to __auto_type. This change prevents the sysfs entry from displaying as __auto_type and ensures it shows as 'auto'. The patch also bypasses an unnecessary permissions check for this specific file. The fix is based on a recent commit in the mm-new branch.
